understanding.project_summary: Confirmed: AutoDocX is a FastAPI-based service that scans local Python projects, parses and analyzes their code structure, stores scan results, compares scans, builds compact project contexts (including an LLM-friendly context) and uses configured LLM providers to generate structured project understanding. Reasonable inference: It is intended as a developer tool to produce machine-readable documentation and project metadata suitable for downstream LLM prompts or documentation generation.
understanding.architecture_overview: Confirmed: Core components include a FastAPI router (app.api.project_routes) mounted from the application entry (app/main.py); scanning and parsing services (app.services.project_scanner, app.services.code_parser, app.services.project_analyzer); persistent storage (app.services.scan_storage) that writes scans under workspace/scans/<project_name>/<scan_id>.json; comparison logic (app.services.scan_comparator); project context builder (app.services.project_context_builder); and an LLM integration layer (app.services.llm.*) with a BaseLLMProvider, concrete OpenAIProvider and AnthropicProvider, and an LLMProviderFactory. Configuration helpers live in app.core.config and Pydantic models are declared in app.models.project. Reasonable inference: At runtime incoming API requests drive the orchestrated use of scanner, analyzer, storage and LLM provider components to produce API responses. Confirmed: ProjectUnderstandingService composes the context builder and LLM factory and uses prompts from app.services.llm.prompts.

execution_flow.title: Application startup and router registration
execution_flow.description: Confirmed: app/main.py creates the FastAPI app, registers project_routes.router (alias project_router) and exposes root and health endpoints (/ and /health). Inference: create_required_directories from app.core.config is likely called on startup (not explicitly shown) to prepare workspace directories.

execution_flow.title: Scan a local project (/api/projects/scan)
execution_flow.description: Confirmed: POST /api/projects/scan is handled by scan_project in app.api.project_routes. That handler uses ProjectScanner.scan_project which enumerates files, calculates SHA-256 file hashes, invokes PythonCodeParser.parse_file for Python files, and calls ProjectAnalyzer.analyze_project to produce project-level relationships. The scan result is returned as ProjectScanResponse and saved via ScanStorage.save_scan.

execution_flow.title: List or retrieve stored scans
execution_flow.description: Confirmed: GET /api/projects/{project_name}/scans returns a list of scan summaries (list_project_scans). GET /api/projects/{project_name}/scans/latest and GET /api/projects/{project_name}/scans/{scan_id} return StoredScanResponse objects using ScanStorage.get_latest_scan and ScanStorage.get_scan respectively. Scans are persisted under workspace/scans/<project_name>/*.json per ScanStorage docstring.

execution_flow.title: Compare two saved scans (/api/projects/{project_name}/compare)
execution_flow.description: Confirmed: POST /api/projects/{project_name}/compare is handled by compare_project_scans which uses ScanComparator.compare_scans to detect added/removed/modified files, functions, classes, methods, routes and internal dependencies. The result is returned as ScanComparisonResponse.

execution_flow.title: Build project context (/api/projects/{project_name}/context)
execution_flow.description: Confirmed: POST /api/projects/{project_name}/context is handled by build_project_context which uses ProjectContextBuilder.build_context to convert a stored scan into a structured context. Confirmed: ProjectContextBuilder supports at least 'detailed' and 'llm' modes (detailed keeps full metadata; llm produces a smaller context). The result is returned as ProjectContextResponse.

execution_flow.title: Generate structured project understanding using an LLM (/api/projects/{project_name}/understand)
execution_flow.description: Confirmed: POST /api/projects/{project_name}/understand is handled by understand_project which delegates to ProjectUnderstandingService.generate_understanding. That service builds the LLM-ready context via ProjectContextBuilder, builds prompts (build_project_understanding_prompt and system prompt constant), instantiates an LLM provider via LLMProviderFactory.create_provider (using provider name, API key and optional model), and calls the provider.generate_json to obtain a parsed JSON understanding. The response is ProjectUnderstandingResponse. Confirmed: LLM providers implemented include OpenAIProvider and AnthropicProvider which both implement BaseLLMProvider.generate_json and provider_name.

execution_flow.title: Configuration and LLM credentials
execution_flow.description: Confirmed: app.core.config exposes get_llm_provider, get_llm_api_key(provider_name) and get_llm_model(provider_name) used by the API handlers/services to select providers and obtain credentials. Reasonable inference: environment variables (dotenv) are used to configure workspace and LLM credentials; credentials are read at request-time through these helpers.

module_responsibilities.responsibility: HTTP API layer: defines FastAPI endpoints for scanning projects, comparing scans, building context, generating LLM-based understanding, and listing/retrieving stored scans. It validates requests/responses using Pydantic models and orchestrates service calls (scanner, context builder, storage, comparator, understanding service). Confirmed: routes include /scan, /{project_name}/compare, /{project_name}/context, /{project_name}/understand, /{project_name}/scans and scan retrieval endpoints.
Application configuration helpers: loads environment variables (dotenv) and exposes functions to create required directories and retrieve LLM provider, API key and model selections.
Application entry point and ASGI app definition: constructs FastAPI app, mounts project routes (project_router) and exposes health endpoints (/ and /health).

Pydantic schema definitions for request and response payloads used across the API (scan requests/responses, stored scan, summaries, context requests/responses, understanding requests/responses, and scan comparison models).

Parses Python source files to extract code structure and metadata. Confirmed: PythonCodeParser.parse_file returns imports, functions, async functions, classes, class methods, FastAPI routes, content hashes and syntax error information for a single file.

High-level project scanner that walks a project directory, filters ignored files, computes file/directory statistics and hashes, invokes PythonCodeParser for Python files and delegates analysis to ProjectAnalyzer to create a complete scan result.

Analyzes parsed files across the project to map modules, discover internal dependencies, collect symbols (functions, classes, methods), resolve FastAPI router prefixes and include_router relationships and produce project-wide statistics and fully resolved endpoint paths.

Converts stored scan results into structured project context. Confirmed: supports at least two modes: 'detailed' (full metadata) and 'llm' (smaller LLM-friendly context). Used by ProjectUnderstandingService and by the /context API endpoint.

Persists and retrieves scan JSON results in a workspace directory (default 'workspace'). Confirmed methods include save_scan, get_scan, get_latest_scan and list_scans. The docstring specifies the storage location workspace/scans/<project_name>/<scan_id>.json.

Compares two stored scans to detect file-level and symbol-level changes (added/removed/modified files, functions, classes, methods, routes and internal dependencies) and produces a structured diff used by comparison API responses.

Orchestrates building an LLM-ready project context and invoking a selected LLM provider to generate parsed JSON project understanding. It uses ProjectContextBuilder, prompt-building helpers and the LLMProviderFactory to instantiate concrete providers.

Abstract base class defining the provider interface every LLM integration must implement: provider_name (property), generate_json(prompt) and the helper parse_json_response to robustly extract JSON from model outputs.
Concrete LLM provider implementation for OpenAI using the Responses API. Implements provider_name and generate_json. Imported by the LLMProviderFactory.
Concrete LLM provider implementation for Anthropic Claude using the Messages API. Implements provider_name and generate_json. Imported by the LLMProviderFactory.
Factory class to normalize provider names and instantiate the appropriate BaseLLMProvider concrete class (OpenAIProvider or AnthropicProvider) given provider name, API key and optional model.

Provides prompt text builders and constants used to construct system and user prompts for the project understanding flow. Confirmed function build_project_understanding_prompt(project_context) exists.

api_overview.purpose: Health / entry endpoint. Confirmed: defined in app/main.py and tagged 'Health'.
Liveness/health check. Confirmed: defined in app/main.py and tagged 'Health'.
Scan, analyze and save a local project. Confirmed: accepts ProjectScanRequest and returns ProjectScanResponse.
Compare two saved project scans and return a structured diff. Confirmed: accepts ScanComparisonRequest and returns ScanComparisonResponse.
Build compact, LLM-ready project context or detailed context from a stored scan. Confirmed: returns ProjectContextResponse.
Generate structured project understanding via the configured LLM provider. Confirmed: returns ProjectUnderstandingResponse and uses configured provider, API key and optional model.
List all saved scans for a given project. Confirmed: returns list[ScanSummaryResponse].
Return the latest saved scan for the project. Confirmed: returns StoredScanResponse.
Retrieve one saved scan by scan_id. Confirmed: returns StoredScanResponse.

key_dependencies.purpose: Invokes ProjectScanner.scan_project to produce a full project scan when handling /api/projects/scan.
Saves scan results and retrieves stored scans for endpoints that list/get scans and for persistence of scan results.
Builds structured project context for the /context endpoint and to prepare context for LLM calls.
Delegates to ProjectUnderstandingService.generate_understanding to produce LLM-generated project understanding for /understand.
Uses ScanComparator.compare_scans to implement the compare API.
Calls PythonCodeParser.parse_file to extract code structure from each Python file during scanning.
Passes parsed files to ProjectAnalyzer.analyze_project to compute project-level relationships and statistics.
Builds the LLM-ready context used as input to the LLM provider.
Creates concrete LLM provider instances (OpenAIProvider or AnthropicProvider) based on provider name and credentials.
Depends on OpenAIProvider to support OpenAI as a concrete provider.
Depends on AnthropicProvider to support Anthropic as a concrete provider.
Registers the project routes with the FastAPI application so endpoints are exposed at runtime.

risks_and_gaps.title: Secrets and credentials exposure / management
risks_and_gaps.description: Confirmed: LLM API keys come from app.core.config.get_llm_api_key(provider_name) and dotenv. Reasonable inference: current code reads keys from env at runtime; there is no evidence of secret rotation, vault integration, or encryption at rest for workspace files. If environment/configuration files are not secured, keys could be exposed.

risks_and_gaps.title: No authentication or authorization on API routes
risks_and_gaps.description: Confirmed: API routes are defined and there is no mention of auth middleware, tokens, or access control in the provided context. Reasonable inference: the service is unauthenticated by default which may be acceptable for local tooling but risky if exposed.

risks_and_gaps.title: LLM response parsing fragility
risks_and_gaps.description: Confirmed: BaseLLMProvider.parse_json_response exists to parse JSON embedded in text. Reasonable inference: parsing free-text LLM responses into strict JSON can be brittle; additional validation and robust error handling are necessary to handle unexpected outputs or hallucinated structures.

risks_and_gaps.title: Synchronous/blocking design and scalability
risks_and_gaps.description: Confirmed: nearly all functions are synchronous (statistics show 0 async functions). Reasonable inference: scanning projects, calling remote LLM APIs, and file I/O may block worker threads. This design can limit concurrency under load and may delay responses; long-running scans could time out in typical server environments.

risks_and_gaps.title: Missing error handling and validation surface in context
risks_and_gaps.description: Confirmed: API handlers and service methods are present but the code-level error handling, retries, and validation logic are not visible in the context. Reasonable inference: failure modes (corrupt files, permission errors, LLM failures) need explicit handling and reporting to avoid 500s or data loss.

risks_and_gaps.title: Storage assumptions and workspace management
risks_and_gaps.description: Confirmed: ScanStorage saves JSON under workspace/scans/<project_name>/<scan_id>.json. Reasonable inference: no details on cleanup, quotas, or concurrency control when multiple requests target the same project. Disk usage and permission errors are potential operational issues.

risks_and_gaps.title: Limited provider support and extensibility notes
risks_and_gaps.description: Confirmed: OpenAI and Anthropic providers are implemented and selected via LLMProviderFactory. Reasonable inference: adding new providers requires implementing BaseLLMProvider and registering it in the factory; the factory's provider normalization behavior and supported names should be documented.

risks_and_gaps.title: Testing, CI, and observability not present in supplied context
risks_and_gaps.description: Confirmed: no tests, logging configuration or CI scripts are visible in the provided files list. Reasonable inference: additional infrastructure documentation and tests should be created to ensure correctness and maintainability.

recommended_document_sections.title: Project Overview
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Explain AutoDocX purpose, capabilities and high-level architecture (scanning, analysis, storage, comparison, LLM-based understanding). Include a short diagram or component list.

recommended_document_sections.title: Getting Started and Deployment
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Steps to install, configure environment variables (LLM keys, workspace path), run create_required_directories, and start the FastAPI app using provided scripts (start.bat, stop.bat, setup_runtime.ps1).

recommended_document_sections.title: API Reference
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Detailed documentation of each endpoint (/api/projects/* and health endpoints), request/response schemas (linking to Pydantic models in app.models.project), expected status codes and example payloads.

recommended_document_sections.title: Data Models and Storage Format
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Document Pydantic models, the structure of stored scan JSON files, storage layout (workspace/scans/<project_name>/<scan_id>.json) and retention/cleanup guidance.

recommended_document_sections.title: LLM Provider Integration
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Describe BaseLLMProvider interface, how to add new providers, LLMProviderFactory behavior, environment configuration (provider name, API key, optional model), and prompt design (PROJECT_UNDERSTANDING_SYSTEM_PROMPT and build_project_understanding_prompt).

recommended_document_sections.title: Scanning and Parsing Details
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Explain ProjectScanner behavior (ignore rules, hashing, file statistics), PythonCodeParser outputs (imports, functions, classes, routes, content hashes and syntax errors) and ProjectAnalyzer responsibilities (dependency mapping and route path resolution).

recommended_document_sections.title: Error Handling and Retries
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Document known failure modes, recommended error handling strategies, retry policies for LLM calls, and how the API surfaces errors to clients.

recommended_document_sections.title: Security and Secrets Management
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Guidance on securing environment variables, recommended secret stores/vaults, least-privilege considerations, and suggestions for adding authentication/authorization to the API.

recommended_document_sections.title: Operational Considerations
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Performance expectations, concurrency and scaling notes (synchronous design), workspace disk usage, permissions, and recommended resource limits or background job patterns for long-running scans.

recommended_document_sections.title: Testing and CI
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Suggested test matrix, unit/integration test targets (parsers, analyzers, providers), and a sample CI pipeline to validate builds and run tests.

recommended_document_sections.title: Extensibility Guide
recommended_document_sections.purpose: How to extend the system: add new LLM providers, support additional file types, change storage backend, or add authentication middleware.
